items.name: Predicting-fraudulent-transactions-in-cryptos
items.description: using the Elliptic Data Set (https://www.kaggle.com/ellipticco/elliptic-data-set) and working to improve on the orignals results by Weber, Mark, et al. "Anti-money laundering in bitcoin: Experimenting with graph convolutional networks for financial forensics." arXiv preprint arXiv:1908.02591 (2019).  We use ML models such as SVM and Random Forest. Oversampling techiques such as SMOTE, ctCAN and GMM were used to address the class imbalance problem in the dataset. Also, we generated node embeddings using DeepWalk and trained ML models on them.

items.name: anti-money-laundering
items.description: The project is based on the analysis of the "IBM Transactions for Anti Money Laundering" dataset published on Kaggle. The task is to implement a model which predicts whether or not a transaction is illicit, using the attribute "Is Laundering" as a label to be predicted.

items.name: AntiMoneyLaundering
items.description: Linear Regression Model analyzing the IBM Transactions for Anti Money Laundering (AML) dataset from Kaggle. Model accurately predicts (RMSE = 0.032)  whether a given bank transaction is an example of money laundering activity.

items.name: csc871-anti-money-laundering-ibm-gnn
items.description: A GNN project on anti-money laundering using IBM's transaction dataset

items.name: aml-transaction-monitoring
items.description: A end-to-end Anti-Money Laundering (AML) analytics project using two real-world Kaggle datasets. Covers SQL-based aggregation, Python risk scoring, and a Power BI dashboard to identify high-risk accounts and suspicious transaction patterns.

items.name: Meezan-Bank-International-Transactions-Dataset
items.description: This dataset contains 15,000 international banking transactions from Meezan Bank. It is useful for data analysis, fraud detection, AML (Anti-Money Laundering), and financial insights.

items.name: aml-detection
items.description: LightGBM classifier for anti-money laundering detection on the IBM AMLworld dataset, combining graph-based and transaction-derived features.

items.name: AI-and-Graph-based-Approaches-for-Anti-Money-Laundering-in-Financial-Networks
items.description: Neighborhood illicit ratios are the dominant signals. We discuss limitations including indirect label dependency on ground-truth transaction labels and dataset specificity.

items.name: money-laundering-detection-aml
items.description: Machine learning based Anti-Money Laundering (AML) detection system using a large-scale synthetic banking transaction dataset for fraud risk monitoring and suspicious activity identification

items.name: Scalable-Anti-Money-Laundering-Detection-Classification-Using-Apache-Spark-and-HDFS
items.description: Money laundering poses a significant threat to global financial systems by disguising illicit funds as legitimate transactions. This project develops a scalable anti-money laundering (AML) detection system using Apache Spark and Hadoop Distributed File System (HDFS) to analyze a large financial transaction dataset.
